pattern: Collecting the distinct digit characters and reading off the second-highest gives the answer, or minus one when fewer than two distinct digits appear.
hints: Only the digit characters matter; ignore the letters entirely.
Distinct digits are what count, so duplicates of the same digit do not create a second-largest.
Gather the set of digits present, and if it has at least two members return the second largest, otherwise return -1.

edge_inputs.description: three distinct digits, second largest is two
only one distinct digit despite repeats
highest digit appears once, second largest below it

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 500))
    alpha = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789'
    return [''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(n))]
